message: Node started: Find the smallest prime p such that there exists an integer n where n^4 + 1 is divisible by p^2. To do this, check primes p in increasing order. For each p, check if there is an n in the range 1 to p-1 such that n^4 + 1 is divisible by p. If such an n exists, check if n^4 + 1 is also divisible by p^2. Note that for n^4 + 1 = 0 mod p to have a solution, p must be 2 or a prime of the form 8k + 1.
